summary: Unused shell:allow-execute capability grants an arbitrary-command-execution primitive with no consumer
Unused clipboard-manager:allow-read-text capability is a needless exfiltration surface
Password generator copy used navigator.clipboard; unreliable in WebView2 origin and failed silently with no feedback
Advertised '/' search-focus shortcut had no keyboard handler
delete_group could delete the default group and orphan entries' group references until next launch
generate_password returned an empty password for non-positive length and risked huge allocation for absurd length
CLAUDE.md falsely claimed the password generator is broken; stale DB-path section, command list, and version
Copied passwords now auto-clear from the clipboard after 60s (conditional, countdown toast); BUG-002 partially reversed (read-text re-added) by explicit decision
Global Space preventDefault breaks Space-activating focused buttons
Master row read via hard-coded WHERE id = 1 while insert does not pin id
Batch delete performs N sequential round-trips, each reloading entries and groups
CSV import assumes a header row; headerless third-party CSV silently drops its first data row
computeStrength duplicated across two components; pre-existing clippy warnings
